main_event: Nine civilian observers at Fort Benning, Georgia observed a round object with a fan-shaped wake that remained stationary for 20 minutes then departed to the southeast at high speed on January 12, 1951 at 1445 EST.
sighted_object: round; fan-shaped wake or trail; stationary for 20 minutes; departed rapidly to SE at high speed; no sound reported
course: Stationary then departed SE at high speed
witness_description: Nine (9) civilian observers at Fort Benning, Georgia; identifying information redacted
interesting_points: Record card checks both 'Was Astronomical' and 'UNIDENTIFIED' — unusual dual conclusion
Object stationary for full 20 minutes before rapid departure — behavior inconsistent with known astronomical phenomena

5-page PDF file contains only 2 pages relevant to this case; pages 2, 4, and 5 contain unrelated bundled documents: Los Alamos meteor note, Project Twinkle cover page, and EAF transmittal letter respectively
9 civilian witnesses is a relatively large civilian group for a Ft. Benning sighting in 1951
